content.problemStatement:
There is a building with 10910^9109 floors labelled 111 to 10910^9109 from the bottom to the top. NNN people would like to move up several floors in the building. Specifically, the iii-th person wants to move from floor AiA_iAi​ to floor BiB_iBi​, where Ai<BiA_i < B_iAi​<Bi​.

There are MMM elevators in the building. An elevator can hold **any** number of people. Initially, at the 000-th second, all elevators are at floor 111.

An elevator takes 111 second to move up 111 floor but cannot move down any floors. On any floor, an elevator may stop so that people may enter or exit the elevator. Each person takes HHH seconds to enter or exit an elevator. Two or more people **cannot** enter or exit the same elevator at the same time; they must wait for their turn.

A person can exit an elevator on any floor, not only floor BiB_iBi​. In particular, a person can switch elevators by exiting on some floor and entering a different elevator on the same floor.

We say that the iii-th person has reached the destination **after** taking HHH seconds to exit an elevator at floor BiB_iBi​.

Find the minimum time needed in seconds until all NNN people have reached their destinations if they enter and exit elevators optimally.

Note: We would recommend Python users to use PYPY/PYPY3 instead of PYTHON/PYTHON3.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains three integers NNN, MMM and HHH — the number of people, the number of elevators and the time taken for a person to enter or exit an elevator.
NNN lines follow. The iii-th line contains two integers AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​, where the iii-th person wants to move from floor AiA_iAi​ to floor BiB_iBi​.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer on a new line, representing the minimum time needed until all NNN people have reached their destinations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases will not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
The sum of MMM over all test cases will not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, a possible sequence of events is given as follows for each elevator.
Elevator 111:

At the 000-th second, elevator 111 starts moving up from floor 111.
At the 222-nd second, elevator 111 stops at floor 333. Person 444 starts entering elevator 111.
At the 444-th second, person 444 finishes entering elevator 111. Elevator 111 continues moving up from floor 333.
At the 666-th second, elevator 111 stops at floor 555. Person 444 starts exiting elevator 111.
At the 888-th second, person 444 finishes exiting elevator 111. Elevator 111 continues moving up from floor 555.

Elevator 222:

At the 000-th second, elevator 222 starts moving up from floor 111.
At the 111-st second, elevator 222 stops at floor 222. Person 222 starts entering elevator 222.
At the 333-rd second, person 222 finishes entering elevator 222. Elevator 222 continues moving up from floor 222.
At the 444-th second, elevator 222 stops at floor 333. Person 222 starts exiting elevator 222.
At the 666-th second, person 222 finishes exiting elevator 222. Elevator 222 continues moving up from floor 333.
At the 888-th second, elevator 222 stops at floor 555. Person 444 starts entering elevator 222.
At the 101010-th second, person 444 finishes entering elevator 222. Person 333 starts entering elevator 222.
At the 121212-th second, person 333 finishes entering elevator 222. Person 555 starts entering elevator 222.
At the 141414-th second, person 555 finishes entering elevator 222. Elevator 222 continues moving up from floor 555.
At the 151515-th second, elevator 222 stops at floor 666. Person 333 starts exiting elevator 222.
At the 171717-th second, person 333 finishes exiting elevator 222. Elevator 222 continues moving up from floor 666.
At the 191919-th second, elevator 222 stops at floor 888. Person 444 starts exiting elevator 222.
At the 212121-st second, person 444 finishes exiting elevator 222. Elevator 222 continues moving up from floor 888.
At the 242424-th second, elevator 222 stops at floor 111111. Person 555 starts exiting elevator 222.
At the 262626-th second, person 555 finishes exiting elevator 222. Elevator 222 continues moving up from floor 111111.

Elevator 333:

At the 000-th second, elevator 333 starts moving up from floor 111.
At the 111111-th second, elevator 333 stops at floor 121212. Person 111 starts entering elevator 333.
At the 131313-th second, person 111 finishes entering elevator 333. Elevator 333 continues moving up from floor 121212.
At the 252525-th second, elevator 333 stops at floor 242424. Person 111 starts exiting elevator 333.
At the 272727-th second, person 111 finishes exiting elevator 333. Elevator 333 continues moving up from floor 242424.

All people reach their destinations by 272727 seconds. Notice that person 444 switched elevators, which is allowed. It can be shown that there is no way for them to reach their destinations by 262626 seconds or fewer.
